memoryArtifacts.headline: The Lindeseie and Limesi Families of Great Britain, c1917
The ancestry of Hugo de Toesni, surnamed de Limesi, via a 1917 publication
memoryArtifacts.description: In 1917 a book entitled "The Lindeseie and Limesi Families of Great Britain, Including the Probates at Sumerset House, London, England, of all Spellings of the Name Lindeseie from 1300-1800, Volume 1", was "privately printed" in "Boston, Massachusetts" by "John William Linzee, A.B., S.B."  This publication is available online (https://archive.org/stream/linzeefamilyofgr01linz#page/n13/mode/2up).  The supposed ancestry of "Hugh de Toesni, surnamed de Limesi"--back to Odin (born about 76 B.C.), King of Asgardia and the first king of Scandinavia--is shown in pedigrees on pages 178-185.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugo de Limesi de Tosny於大約0990年出生在法國豪-諾門迪帕斯 德 考時，他的父親Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches31歲，母親Judith de Bayeux29歲。他於大約1039年與Christine de Conteville在法國諾門迪結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1060年在他的家鄉享年72歲。

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tosny
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean Pierre.
Americanized form of any of various surnames ultimately derived from the Latin personal name Antonius (see Anthony ), for example Greek Antoniou and Italian Toni .
French: from the personal name Tony, a pet form of Antonin (from Latin Antoninus, a derivative of Antonius; see Anthony ).

variants.name: Anthony
meanings: English and West Indian (mainly Antigua and Barbuda, Saint Lucia, and Trinidad and Tobago, also Dutch Caribbean): from the personal name Anthony, Latin Antonius, which, with its variants and cognates, is one of the commonest personal names in Europe. Spellings with -h-, which first appear in English in the 16th century and in French (as Anthoine) at about the same time, are due to the erroneous belief that the name derives from Greek anthos ‘flower’. The popularity of the personal name in Christendom is largely due to the cult of the Egyptian hermit Saint Anthony ( AD 251–356), who in his old age gathered a community of hermits around him, and for that reason is regarded by some as the founder of monasticism. It was further increased by the fame of Saint Anthony of Padua (1195–1231), who long enjoyed a great popular cult and who is believed to help people find lost things. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and derivatives (patronymics) from other languages, for example Greek patronymic Antoniades , Italian Antoni , Polish Antoniewicz , Croatian and Serbian Antonović (see Antonovich ) and Antunović; see also below. The name Anthony is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Antony .

German, Flemish, and French (mainly Alsace): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from local equivalents of the Latin personal name Antonius, from its genitive form Antoni(i). In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the German, Dutch, French, and Slovak cognates Antoni 1 and Antony 2.
History: John Anthony of Hampstead, Middlesex, England (now part of north London) migrated to Boston, MA, in 1634. By 1640 he had moved to Providence, RI, where his descendants are still established.

nameMeanings.name: Hugo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of   Hugh  , used throughout the Middle Ages in official documents, and now enjoying a revival as a given name.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugo de Limesi de Tosny was born about 0990, in Pays de Caux, Upper Normandy, France, his father, Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches, was 31 and his mother, Judith de Bayeux, was 29. He married Christine de Conteville about 1039, in Normandy, France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died about 1060, in his hometown, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugo de Limesi de Tosny est né environ 0990, à Pays de Caux, Haute-Normandie, France, son père, Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Judith de Bayeux, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Christine de Conteville environ 1039, à Normandie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1060, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 72 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hugo de Limesi de Tosny um 0990 in Pays de Caux, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches 31 und seine Mutter Judith de Bayeux 29. Er hat um 1039 in Normandie, Frankreich Christine de Conteville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1060 im Alter von 72 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hugo de Limesi de Tosny è nato il circa 0990 nel Pays de Caux, Haute-Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Judith de Bayeux, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Christine de Conteville circa 1039, nel Normandie, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1060, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 72 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugo de Limesi de Tosnyが約0990年にフランスハウテ-ノーマンディペイズ ド カウックスで生まれた時，父Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conchesは31歳，母Judith de Bayeuxは29歳であった。約1039年にフランスノーマンディでChristine de Contevilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1060年に故郷で72歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugo de Limesi de Tosny(이)가 대략 0990년 프랑스 하테-노먼디 파이스 데 카스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Judith de Bayeux(은)는 29세였다. 그는 대략 1039년 프랑스 노먼디에서 Christine de Conteville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1060년 그의 고향에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hugo de Limesi de Tosny nasceu aproximadamente 0990, em Pays de Caux, Haute-Normandie, França, seu pai, Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Judith de Bayeux, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Christine de Conteville aproximadamente 1039, em Normandie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1060, em sua cidade natal, com 72 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugo de Limesi de Tosny родился около 0990 г. в местности пеис де каукс, хауте-норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Judith de Bayeux, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Christine de Conteville около 1039 г. в местности норманди, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1060 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 72 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hugo de Limesi de Tosny nació aproximadamente 0990, en Pays de Caux, Haute-Normandie, Francia, su papá, Raoul II Seigneur de Tosny et de Conches, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Judith de Bayeux, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Christine de Conteville aproximadamente 1039, en Normandie, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1060, en su pueblo, a la edad de 72 años.
